description_long: This house features a long rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior walls are clad in a warm, textured material resembling wood or bamboo, supported by vertical and horizontal structural beams. The front facade includes a covered entryway with steps leading up to an open porch area, partially sheltered by an extended roof overhang. The side elevations display a series of small, regularly spaced windows and solid wall sections, while the rear has a single small window centered on the wall. The roof extends well beyond the walls, providing significant shade and protection from the elements. The overall design is simple yet functional, with a focus on practicality and weather resistance.
description: A long, rectangular house with a steep gable roof, wood-textured walls, and a covered front porch. The design is practical, with extended roof overhangs and minimal window openings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered entryway, steps, and open porch beneath the extended gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the long, rectangular profile, small windows, and the deep overhang of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single centered window and the continuation of the steep gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the elongated structure and window arrangement.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with small, regularly spaced windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with steps, open porch, and extended roof overhang supported by columns
